title: Capsicum cornutum
taxonomy: Capsicum cornutum is a species within the Capsicum genus, which is part of the nightshade family (Solanaceae). This classification is based on its botanical characteristics, such as its fruit type and growth habits. The Capsicum genus is further divided into several species, including Capsicum annuum, Capsicum frutescens, and Capsicum chinense, among others. Capsicum cornutum is a lesser-known species within this genus, and its classification may not be as well-documented as other species. Further research may be needed to fully understand its classification and place within the Capsicum genus.

intro: Capsicum cornutum, also known as horned pepper or horned chili, is a species of flowering plant in the Capsicum genus. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from pain and inflammation, as well as its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cardiovascular disease and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Capsicum cornutum include capsaicinoids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic benefits. Capsicum cornutum is typically prepared as a tincture, tea, or capsule, and is often used in traditional medicine to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, fibromyalgia, and other musculoskeletal disorders.
